HVI.name: Hyperscale Vector Index
HVI.description: HVI keeps vector data on SSD while the navigation graph lives in RAM. This separation allows billion-scale search without proportionally large RAM. Optimized for high recall with a low memory footprint (Data Greatness in Memory - DGM ratio of 2%)
best_for: Use cases prioritizing high accuracy with low latency at massive scale with mid-high filtering
limitations: not optimized for <20% selective filters (where only a small fraction of the corpus is eligible for vector search)

storage_model.requirement: Full index must reside in RAM to avoid severe latency degradation

CVI.primary_signal: Structured workloads where filters are narrowly scoped — typically <20% selective, meaning a small fraction of the corpus is eligible for vector search before the ANN step
CVI.description: CVI combines a traditional GSI for structured field filtering with the vector similarity component. It uses 'Filter-First' logic, where metadata conditions are applied at the index level before the vector search runs, dramatically reducing the search space for the ANN step.

performance_nuance.details: Entire index should reside in RAM to maintain performance. At massive scale, RAM costs become the primary limiting factor.

best_for: Workloads with complex, multi-layered filters (e.g., location, price, amenities) that narrow results to a small subset (<20%)
Multi-tenant architectures with strong per-tenant isolation (e.g., filtering by customer_id or kb_id)
Search scenarios where customers already run regular SQL queries and need seamless integration
limitations: Not viable at billion-scale without proportionally large RAM budgets
Poor fit when filter is >20% selective — a large fraction of the corpus remains eligible for vector search, so the pre-filter doesn't meaningfully shrink the ANN search space

Search Vector Index.primary_signal: Small-to-medium scale Hybrid Search requiring native lexical relevance (BM25, fuzzy matching, stemming)
Search Vector Index.description: Integrates vector similarity search into Couchbase's Full Text Search service. It enables queries that combine lexical matching with semantic intent in a single unified index.

performance_nuance.details: As vector count approaches the 100M range, memory pressure increases significantly.

best_for: Applications relying on text relevance plus semantic similarity at moderate scale (<100M vectors)
Use cases requiring autocomplete, fuzzy matching, and geospatial constraints integrated with vectors
Operational simplicity for D2C platforms or small-scale RAG apps
limitations: Not supported on Windows; Linux and MacOS only

Hybrid.architecture: Orchestrated use of HVI (for billion-scale vectors) and FTS Service (for lexical keywords)
Hybrid.primary_signal: Large-scale Hybrid Search — Text + Semantic at scale exceeding FTS limits
Hybrid.description: HVI handles the billion-scale vector workload while FTS handles keyword indexing. Results from both systems are combined and re-ranked at the application layer.
best_for: Datasets exceeding the FTS memory ceiling (~100M vectors) that still require high-precision text matching
Billion-scale RAG or Search applications needing both lexical precision and semantic depth
limitations: Higher operational complexity due to orchestrating two services
Requires manual re-ranking logic at the application layer

decision_pivots.name: Temporal Scale & Projected Growth
decision_pivots.principle: Never assume current document counts are static.
decision_pivots.scaling_rule: If the corpus is 10M today but projected to exceed 100M within 24–36 months, reconsider FTS-only options.
decision_pivots.infrastructure_risk: Migrating from FTS (Search API) to GSI (SQL++ syntax) can require a significant application rewrite.
decision_pivots.decision_intent: Account for forward-looking scale to avoid re-architecture under production pressure.

decision_pivots.name: Filter Selectivity Logic
decision_pivots.principle: Measure how much data remains after structured filtering before vector search.

high_selectivity.rationale: If structured filters (e.g., WHERE tenant_id = 'X') eliminate 80%+ of data, pre-filtering prevents unnecessary vector comparisons.

low_selectivity.rationale: When filters are weak, simultaneous graph traversal is more efficient than scanning a large minimally reduced filtered index.

decision_pivots.decision_intent: Align index strategy with real filter reduction behavior, not assumed filtering strength.

decision_pivots.name: Search Persona (Lexical vs. Semantic)
decision_pivots.principle: Identify whether lexical precision or semantic similarity drives user value.
decision_pivots.decision_intent: Match index capabilities to user-facing search expectations.

decision_pivots.name: The Scale Bottleneck (CVI → HVI Pivot)
decision_pivots.principle: Avoid RAM-bound architectures when memory pressure becomes structural.
decision_pivots.risk_condition: CVI begins page swapping due to RAM constraints.
decision_pivots.recommendation: Pivot to HVI using a disk-centric DGM model (~2% memory residency).
decision_pivots.decision_intent: Prevent severe latency degradation caused by memory thrashing.

ANN_Tuning_Parameters.purpose: Operational tuning levers that directly influence recall, latency, throughput, and build performance in production environments.

parameters.name: nList
parameters.description: Controls the number of partitions (clusters) in the index.
parameters.tuning_intent: Balance build-time cost against runtime efficiency.

parameters.name: nProbe
parameters.description: Determines how many partitions are searched per query.
parameters.tuning_intent: Adjust recall sensitivity relative to acceptable latency budgets.

parameters.name: Reranking
parameters.description: When persist_full_vector is enabled (HVI only), exact vector distances are used to re-sort ANN results.
parameters.tuning_intent: Use selectively when precision outweighs latency and IO cost.

parameters.name: Train_list
parameters.description: Size of the training sample used to generate the Codebook.
parameters.tuning_intent: Optimize training quality relative to acceptable provisioning time.

hazards.name: Codebook Drift
hazards.problem: Codebook is trained on a static sample and may become non-representative over time.
hazards.cause: Incremental data updates shift the underlying distribution without retraining the codebook.
hazards.impact: Silent recall degradation with no explicit system warning.

mitigation.recommended_action: Establish scheduled 'drop and recreate' retraining intervals based on data growth or distribution shift.

hazards.name: Memory Pressure (FTS)
hazards.problem: Index may not fit into available RAM even if under the nominal 100M document guideline.
hazards.cause: Memory-constrained environments or competing workloads reduce effective RAM headroom.

mitigation.recommended_strategy: Consider Hyperscale (HVI) for high-scale workloads operating under constrained RAM budgets.

hazards.name: Dimensions & Metrics Mismatch
hazards.problem: Vector dimensions do not exactly match model output dimensions.
hazards.impact: Data may be silently ignored or rejected, leading to incomplete recall.

mitigation.recommended_action: Validate vector dimensionality against the embedding model specification before index creation.

diagnostic_prompts.name: Temporal Volume
diagnostic_prompts.objective: Assess projected scale over a multi-year horizon.
diagnostic_prompts.question: You have 10M vectors now; based on your roadmap, where will this volume be in 3 years?

diagnostic_prompts.name: Selectivity Pressure
diagnostic_prompts.objective: Measure filter reduction strength before vector search.
diagnostic_prompts.question: When you apply filters (like Category or Tenant ID), does that narrow the pool to under 20% of data, or are you searching broadly?

diagnostic_prompts.name: Lexical Requirement
diagnostic_prompts.objective: Determine whether lexical relevance is required alongside semantic similarity.
diagnostic_prompts.question: Do you need typos handling and fuzzy keyword matching, or is this purely semantic 'concepts-only' search?

diagnostic_prompts.name: Technical Identification
diagnostic_prompts.question: What are the specific output dimensions and the required similarity metric (Cosine, L2, or Dot Product) of your model?
